question:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the word ‘stimulate’ from the following sentence.
His jovial nature energises and charms the whole auditorium.
explanation:
• **Stimulate**: To raise levels of physiological or nervous activity in (the body or any of its parts). (Synonym: Energises). ⚡
• **Energises**: To give vitality and enthusiasm to. ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment.
The invitation card very clearly mentions that the <u>entry in the show will be</u> closed at 6 p.m.
options: entry about the show will be
entry over the show will be
entry on the show will be
entry to the show will be
answer: entry to the show will be
explanation: • **Preposition**: The correct idiomatic phrase is 'entry **to**' an event or place. 🎟️

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
There was a story about a kind tree and a little boy. He used to sit _________ the shade of the tree.
explanation: • **Usage**: One sits '**in**' the shade of an object, because the shade is a defined area of reduced light. 🌳⛱️

question:
The following sentence has been divided into parts. Select the part that contains the error. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’.
The forest ranger always / treats all the wild animals / in his area very kindly.
explanation: • The sentence is grammatically correct. It uses the simple present tense to describe a habitual action. ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM to substitute the underlined word.
The <u>evasive</u> nature of the politician is what is making her a poor choice.
explanation:
• **Evasive**: Tending to avoid commitment or self-revelation, especially by responding only indirectly. 🌫️
• **Truthful**: Telling or expressing the truth; honest. (Antonym). 🗣️✅

question:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.
**Impress**
explanation: • **Impress**: To make someone feel admiration and respect; to influence someone's opinion or feelings. (Synonym: Influence). ✨

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
**In the long run**
explanation: • **In the long run**: Over a long period of time; eventually; in the end. 🛤️

question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
explanation:
• **Incorrect spelling**: Endavour. ❌
• **Correct spelling**: **Endeavour**. ✅ (Meaning an attempt to achieve a goal).

question:
Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment.
The division of labour is the second strategy <u>to possess money</u>.
explanation: • **Context**: In financial strategy, the goal is typically '**to build wealth**' rather than just 'possessing money'. 💰📈

question:
Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word and select its correct spelling.
She needs to visit a <u>phyziotherapist</u>.
explanation: • **Correct spelling**: **Physiotherapist**. ✅ (Note the 's' instead of 'z'). 🏥

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
**Capture**
explanation:
• **Capture**: To take into one's possession or control by force. ⛓️
• **Liberate**: To set someone free from imprisonment, slavery, or oppression. (Antonym). 🔓

question:
Select the word that is similar in meaning to the underlined word.
It was difficult to find a police officer who was not <u>partial</u>.
explanation: • **Partial**: Favoring one side in a dispute above the other. (Synonym: Biased). ⚖️❌

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
**Armed to the teeth**
options: Decide to continue in spite of difficulties
explanation: • **Armed to the teeth**: Fully or heavily armed with weapons. ⚔️🛡️

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
**Hesitate**
explanation:
• **Hesitate**: To pause before saying or doing something, usually through uncertainty. 🤨
• **Resolve**: To settle or find a solution to; to decide firmly on a course of action. (Antonym). ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
**Reluctant**
explanation:
• **Reluctant**: Unwilling and hesitant; disinclined. 🚫
• **Willing**: Ready, eager, or prepared to do something. (Antonym). ✅

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 1) Tourists Travelling(1) _______ Jammu and Kashmir have another attraction...
explanation: • **Preposition**: Tourists travel '**to**' a destination (Jammu and Kashmir). 🗺️

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 2) ...claimed to be the only one such post office in the world – is (2) ______ on an intricately carved maroon houseboat.
explanation: • **Passive Voice**: The post office 'is **built**' on a houseboat. It describes the state/location. 🏠

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 3) This post office lets you (3) _____ all the regular postal services available.
explanation: • **Collocation**: To '**avail**' services means to make use of them. 📮

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 4) These pictures reach wherever these letters are posted to and (4) _______ promote Kashmir.
explanation: • **Adverb**: '**Consequently**' (as a result) explains how the movement of letters leads to the promotion of tourism. 📣

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 5) The post office’s houseboat has two small rooms – one (5) _____ as the office...
explanation: • **Serving**: Functioning as or fulfilling the role of. The room 'serves' as the office. 🏢
